risks.title: Attaching file-level imports to every element may inflate index size or duplicate data.
risks.mitigation: Deduplicate imports per file, keep field shape stable, and validate output size impact in a fresh clone.

risks.title: usedBy could point to files rather than exact elements, creating misleading element-level semantics.
risks.mitigation: Document file-level reverse dependency semantics and keep entries normalized as file/import/line until element-resolution is separately designed.

risks.title: Source-header opt-in mode may still modify too many files or produce noisy headers.
risks.mitigation: Group by file, merge headers once, deduplicate content, and add CRLF/existing-comment regression tests.

risks.title: Changing PipelineState can affect generator or plugin type contracts.
risks.mitigation: Make imports/calls additive fields, keep existing state fields unchanged, and run build plus focused generator tests.

decision_records.decision: Fix relationship propagation by exposing extracted relationships on PipelineState and enriching ElementData before projections.
decision_records.rationale: This preserves the existing single-pass extractor and avoids a duplicate scanner.

decision_records.decision: Treat imports and usedBy as file-level semantic fields for this workorder.
decision_records.rationale: The current extractor produces file-level import relationships; exact symbol-level resolution is a separate larger problem.

decision_records.decision: Keep source headers opt-in and make them one merged projection per file.
decision_records.rationale: Default populate must remain non-mutating, while header mode should avoid repeated writes and malformed comments.
